data.title: CEO of Capital One warns Trump's 10% interest rate cap on credit cards could lead to recession, impacting $6 trillion consumer spending.
data.description: Capital One Financial Corp. CEO Richard Fairbank expressed concerns over President Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates, warning of potential consequences during the company's fourth-quarter earnings call. Fairbank stated that such a move could restrict consumer access to credit and destabilize the economy. He emphasized that implementing price controls would not make credit more affordable but rather limit its availability for consumers across different credit profiles. Fairbank highlighted the significant role of consumer credit in the U.S. economy, noting that 70% of GDP is driven by consumer spending, with $6 trillion attributed to such expenditure. He indicated that banks would likely have to slash credit lines, restrict accounts, and limit new originations if the proposal were enforced, potentially leading to a recession.

data.title: Webster Financial (NYSE: WBS) to release Q4 earnings Jan. 23. Analysts predict $1.53 EPS and revenue at $731.66M. Stock closes at $66.22.
data.description: Webster Financial Corporation (WBS), listed on NYSE, is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, Jan. 23. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $1.53 per share, showing an increase from $1.43 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus forecast for the company's quarterly revenue is $731.66 million, compared to $660.98 million in the previous year. On Dec. 10, Webster decreased its prime lending rate to 6.75%. Closing at $66.22 per share on Thursday, Webster Financial saw a 0.5% rise. For more detailed information, including analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.
